verdict_reasoning: B2M (beta-2 microglobulin) is the non-covalent light chain of MHC class I complexes. It is a small ~12 kDa protein with no transmembrane domain of its own, but it assembles in the ER with the MHC-I heavy chain (HLA-A/B/C) and peptide to form a stable trimeric complex that is co-trafficked to the cell surface. At the plasma membrane, B2M occupies the extracellular face as an integral structural component of the MHC-I complex and is fully accessible from outside the cell. Antibodies and other binders targeting B2M on the cell surface are well-documented (e.g., anti-B2M antibodies used in flow cytometry on intact non-permeabilized cells, and therapeutic antibodies targeting surface B2M in myeloma). This is distinct from the shed/secreted soluble form circulating in serum; the surface-associated pool is stably anchored via the MHC-I heavy chain. Fits 'stable_complex_partner' — no membrane anchor of its own, but co-trafficked as part of a constitutively surface-displayed complex.
